detaileddescription.para: Use the XCAN_IXR_* constants defined in xcan_l.h to create the bit-mask to enable interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
is the mask to enable. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCAN_IXR_* bits defined in xcan_l.h.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCAN_IXR_* constants defined in xcan_l.h to create the bit-mask to disable interrupt(s).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
is the mask to disable. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCAN_IXR_* bits defined in xcan_l.h.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCAN_IXR_* constants defined in xcan_l.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This routine returns interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCAN_IXR_* constants defined in xcan_l.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The value stored in Interrupt Status Register.

detaileddescription.para: Every bit set in Interrupt Status Register indicates that a specific type of interrupt is occurring, and this function clears one or more interrupts by writing a bit mask to Interrupt Clear Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
is the mask to clear. Bit positions of 1 will be cleared. Bit positions of 0 will not change the previous interrupt status. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCAN_IXR_* bits defined in xcan_l.h.

briefdescription.para: This routine is the interrupt handler for the CAN driver.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the interrupt status from the ISR, determines the source of the interrupts, calls according callbacks, and finally clears the interrupts.

Application beyond this driver is responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XCan_SetHandler() during initialization phase. An example delivered with this driver demonstrates how this could be done.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ----------------------- --------------------------- XCAN_HANDLER_SEND XCan_SendRecvHandler XCAN_HANDLER_RECV XCan_SendRecvHandler XCAN_HANDLER_ERROR XCan_ErrorHandler XCAN_HANDLER_EVENT XCan_EventHandler

HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- XCAN_HANDLER_SEND A frame transmitted by a call to XCan_Send() has been sent successfully.
XCAN_HANDLER_RECV A frame has been received and is sitting in the RX FIFO.
XCAN_HANDLER_ERROR An error interrupt is occurring.
XCAN_HANDLER_EVENT Any other kind of interrupt is occurring.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCan instance to be worked on.
specifies which handler is to be attached.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.
